methods.description: This method involves the preparation and analysis of samples from boreholes for palynological studies, which include the examination of palynomorphs and other particulate organic matter (POM) to determine the age and environmental conditions of the sedimentary layers.

steps.description: A total of 26 samples from Bh 1, 31 samples from Bh 2, 27 samples from Bh 3 and the single sample from Bh 4, were selected for palynological analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: select samples from boreholes for palynological analysis

steps.description: Samples were processed following standard palynological preparation methods at the laboratory of the Geological Survey of Denmark and Greenland (GEUS).
steps.searchable_summary: process samples using standard palynological preparation methods

steps.description: The palynological samples were analyzed for other particulate organic matter (POM) following the classification and categories of Tyson.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples for particulate organic matter using Tyson's classification

steps.description: The various palynofacies' components were point-counted on unoxidized slides from sieved residues (20 µm).
steps.searchable_summary: perform point-counting of palynofacies components on unoxidized slides

steps.description: Both palynomorphs and other palynodebris were more closely examined in slides from oxidized residues.
steps.searchable_summary: examine palynomorphs and palynodebris in oxidized residue slides

steps.description: The records listed in Tables 1-3 sum up the observations from at least 300 counts of various types of organic particles observed during several traverses on different parts of the examined slides.
steps.searchable_summary: summarize observations from point-count data into tables
